title: Can India Afford to Quit Coal?
summary: India is the world’s biggest case study for decarbonization, with a coal-reliant grid and 1.4 million people who depend on it. The world’s most populous country has an outsized impact on global climate goals, as well as outsized hurdles on the way to getting there. The scale of the challenge would …

title: The Battery That Breaks Its Own Rules
summary: There's a quirk in physics that most engineers never have to worry about: the bigger a conventional battery gets, the longer it takes to charge. Obvious, really. More capacity, more time. It's one of those rules so intuitive you never bother to question it. Dr. James Quach questioned it. Quach, who…

title: Wealthy consumers are turning to jewelry as an investment, especially colored gemstones
summary: Lofty gold prices and market volatility are pushing wealthy consumers toward jewelry, especially rare colored gemstones, as handbags lose appeal.

title: Nuclear in the Spotlight amid Oil, Gas Crunch
summary: Nuclear energy has been making a comeback recently as one of the low-carbon baseload generation options. This comeback is set to accelerate amid the Middle Eastern crisis and the oil and gas squeeze it is causing. The only problem is that nuclear capacity takes a while to build. “It was a strategic…

title: Big Oil Flocks to Alaska in Record-Setting Petroleum Lease Sale
summary: The first lease sale in the National Petroleum Reserve-Alaska in seven years became the most successful auction in the area ever, as oil majors bid on hundreds of tracts, signaling they haven’t given up on Alaska’s petroleum resources despite development and court challenges. title: David Zaslav’s WBD-Paramount deal payout highlights new 'golden parachutes' for CEOs
summary: WBD CEO David Zaslav could see a payout of more than $800 million after the Paramount Skydance deal.
